title: selaginella firmuloides
intro: Selaginella firmuloides, also known as 'false maidenhair fern', is a species of flowering plant in the Selaginellaceae family, commonly found in tropical and subtropical regions. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, which help to treat various health issues such as fever, cough, and skin infections. The plant is rich in nutrients and has been found to have several health benefits, including antioxidant, anti-cancer, and immunomodulatory effects, which help to protect against cell damage and promote overall well-being. In herbal preparations, Selaginella firmuloides is often used in the form of teas, infusions, and tinctures, and is also used in traditional folk medicine to treat various ailments.
